message:
fix(daemon): pass socket path when auto-starting daemon

When the daemon client auto-starts the xf daemon process, it now passes
the --socket flag with the configured socket path. This ensures the
daemon listens on the same Unix socket that the client will attempt to
connect to.

Previously, if a custom socket path was configured via XF_SOCKET env
or config, the spawned daemon would use its default socket path while
the client expected the custom one, causing connection failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -202,9 +202,14 @@ impl DaemonClient {
         let exe = std::env::current_exe()?;
 
         // Spawn the daemon as a background process
-        // The daemon command should be: xf daemon start
+        // Pass the socket path to ensure daemon listens on the same socket client expects
         let child = Command::new(&exe)
-            .args(["daemon", "start"])
+            .args([
+                "daemon",
+                "start",
+                "--socket",
+                &self.config.socket_path.to_string_lossy(),
+            ])
             .stdin(Stdio::null())
             .stdout(Stdio::null())
             .stderr(Stdio::null())
